title: Cisco Port Security
description: Learn to secure switch access ports using port security, including MAC address limits, sticky learning, violation actions, aging timers, and verification commands.

tasks.prompt: Configure interface FastEthernet0/5 as an access port (not a trunk).
tasks.explanation: Port security is only supported on access ports. The interface must be in access mode before enabling port security features.

tasks.prompt: Enable port security on the current interface.
tasks.explanation: Port security restricts inbound traffic on a port to a limited set of MAC addresses, preventing unauthorized device connections.

tasks.prompt: Set the maximum number of secure MAC addresses allowed on this port to 2.
tasks.explanation: The maximum command limits how many MAC addresses can be learned or statically configured on a secured port.

tasks.prompt: Enable sticky MAC address learning on this port.
tasks.explanation: Sticky learning dynamically learns MAC addresses and writes them to the running configuration as secure entries, surviving reboots when saved.

tasks.prompt: Manually configure secure MAC address 00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E on VLAN 10 for this port.
tasks.explanation: Static secure MAC addresses are manually bound to a port and VLAN, ensuring only the specified device can connect.

tasks.prompt: Set the port security violation mode to shutdown (err-disable the port on violation).
tasks.explanation: Shutdown mode places the port in err-disabled state when a violation occurs, requiring manual intervention to restore connectivity.

tasks.prompt: Set the port security violation mode to restrict (drop traffic and log violations).
tasks.explanation: Restrict mode drops offending traffic and increments a violation counter while keeping the port administratively up.

tasks.prompt: Enable port security aging with a time-based aging period of 120 minutes.
tasks.explanation: Aging removes inactive secure MAC addresses after the configured time period, freeing slots for new devices on dynamic ports.

tasks.prompt: Configure port security aging to include statically configured secure MAC addresses.
tasks.explanation: By default, only dynamically learned addresses age out. The 'aging static' option also ages manually configured secure MAC entries.

tasks.prompt: Display port security status and secure MAC addresses for interface FastEthernet0/5.
tasks.explanation: This command shows the maximum MAC count, current secure addresses, violation mode, and any active security violations on the port.
